About: It is about a dog sport involving a dog pulling a cart or sled loaded with weight a short distance.
Affects: It affects the fitness and wellbeing of dogs.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with carting dog breeds.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the sled sports discipline.
ConsistsOf: It consists of dogs pulling a cart or sled loaded with weight.
HasA: It has dogs pulling a cart or sled.
It has a short distance for pulling.
It has dirt/gravel, grass, carpet, or snow as pulling surfaces.
It has classes sorted by dog weight.
It has a specially constructed freighting harness.
It has a set time frame for pulling.
It has additional weight added after each round.
It has various sanctioning organizations.
It has rules for competition.
It has a risk of physical injury.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by dogs pulling a cart or sled loaded with weight.
It is characterized by a modern adaptation of freighting.
It is characterized by a short distance for pulling.
It is characterized by various pulling surfaces.
It is characterized by classes sorted by dog weight.
It is characterized by a freighting harness designed to distribute weight.
It is characterized by a set time frame for pulling.
It is characterized by additional weight being added to the vehicle in subsequent rounds.
It is characterized by being open to any dog regardless of breed, size or gender.
It is characterized by controversy regarding animal welfare.
HeldIn: It was held in North America.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the Klondike Gold Rush.
It was influenced by observations from Jack London's time in the Yukon.
IsA: It is a modern adaptation of freighting.
It is a positive outlet for active canines.
KnownFor: It is known for a dog pulling a cart or sled loaded with weight.
It is known for its history dating to the Klondike Gold Rush.
It is known for its objectives of pulling the most weight.
It is known for being open to all dog breeds.
It is known for controversy regarding animal welfare.
MemberOf: It is a member of various organizations.
It is a member of the sled sports discipline.
OccurredIn: It occurred in 6 countries including the United Kingdom, Mexico, Puerto Rico, Philippines, Ireland, and the United States.
PartOf: It is part of sled sports.
UsedAs: It is used as a means of entertainment.
It is used as a positive outlet for active canines.
UsedFor: It is used for pulling a cart or sled loaded with weight.
It is used for freighting gear and passengers.
It is used for mail delivery.
It is used for trialing sled dogs.
It is used for improving the fitness and wellbeing of dogs.
It is used for improving the bond with their dog.
UsedIn: It is used in dog sports.
It is used in sled sports.
UsedTo: It is used to move cargo.
It is used to meet minimum standards of strength and speed.
It is used to pull the car or 16 feet within a set time frame.
It is used to add additional weight to the vehicle.
It is used to prepare dogs for dog fighting.
WonBy: It was won by the dog who pulls the most weight.
WrittenIn: It was written in Jack London's 1903 book The Call of the Wild.
